---
title: "Data Analysis"
---

The data-analysis page tracks finite phase-coordinate proofs and exploratory benchmark fixtures for periodic synthetic data. `APPD-B0001` compares coil closure ranking with an autocorrelation baseline on a deterministic known-period signal. `APPD-B0002` extends this to clean, noisy, aliased, and multi-period cases with coil closure, autocorrelation, and periodogram-style baselines.

The multi-period fixture is intentionally ambiguous; it is a warning against overclaiming, not a real-data usefulness claim.

::: {.learning-unit}

## Case-Study Frame

The circular address space is a phase coordinate. The repeated action is advancing through a period. The useful question is whether coil closure gives an interpretable descriptor for periodic data, and that question still requires benchmarks against ordinary baselines.

:::

<div class="warning-box">
Benchmark fixtures are executable references only. They are not proofs of real-data usefulness, statistical optimality, or superiority over ordinary baselines.
</div>

## What To Notice

- The Lean-proved seed covers finite phase-coordinate indexing.
- Python fixtures test deterministic periodic cases.
- Baseline comparisons are required before any usefulness claim.

::: {.checkpoint-box}

## Checkpoint

Why does a clean synthetic periodic fixture not prove that a method will work on real noisy data?

:::

## Source Trail

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="APPD-T0001"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="APPD-T0002"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="APPD-T0003"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="APPD-T0004"></div>

<p data-paper-ref="papers/applications/PAPER_APP_01_COIL_DATA_ANALYSIS.md">Paper source: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/applications/PAPER_APP_01_COIL_DATA_ANALYSIS.md">Coil Data Analysis</a></p>

<script type="module" src="../../widgets/shared/widget_base.js"></script>
